items.title: Cathie Wood Goes Bargain Hunting. Here's 1 "Magnificent Seven" Stock She Just Bought on the DeepSeek Dip.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has been buying Amazon (AMZN) shares amid the recent market sell-off triggered by concerns over a Chinese AI startup called DeepSeek. The article argues that Wood's decision to buy the dip in Amazon is a smart move, as the company's investments in AI infrastructure are already paying off.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article argues that Amazon's investments in AI infrastructure, particularly through its $8 billion investment in Anthropic, are already bearing fruit, with AWS accelerating both revenue and profit growth. The author believes that investors are focusing too much on Amazon's spending and not giving enough credit to the growth the company has already witnessed.

Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has been adding over 120,000 shares worth more than $28 million to five of its exchange-traded funds, including ARK Next Generation Internet, indicating a positive sentiment towards the fund's holdings.

items.title: This 6%-Yielding Dividend Stock Hits Its Target for the 19th Year in a Row
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Enbridge, a pipeline and utility company, has achieved its annual financial guidance for 19 consecutive years, demonstrating the stability of its business model. The company is well-positioned for continued growth, with investments in organic expansion projects and strategic acquisitions that are expected to drive EBITDA and distributable cash flow growth in the coming years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Enbridge's consistent financial performance, stable business model, and growth prospects, indicating a positive outlook for the company.

items.title: 3 Reasons to Buy Toast Stock Like There's No Tomorrow
items.author: Jennifer Saibil
items.description: Toast, a cloud-based AI company serving the restaurant industry, is seeing strong growth and profitability. With a large market opportunity, international expansion, and new product offerings, Toast stock is a compelling buy despite its recent stock gains.

items.title: Nvidia's Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Nearly a Year. Here's What History Says Happens Next.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Nvidia's stock is currently trading at a historically low valuation, but the company's largest clients are expected to significantly increase their AI-related spending in the coming years, which bodes well for Nvidia's business and stock performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that Nvidia's stock is currently undervalued and that the company's business outlook remains strong, with its largest clients expected to significantly increase their AI-related spending in the coming years.

The article mentions that Meta Platforms is one of Nvidia's largest clients and is expected to significantly increase its capital expenditures in 2025, which is seen as a positive for Nvidia's business.
The article states that Alphabet, another major Nvidia client, is also expected to increase its capital expenditures in 2025, which is viewed as a positive for Nvidia's business.

The article notes that Amazon, one of Nvidia's largest clients, is expected to have around $100 billion in capital expenditures in 2025, which is seen as a positive for Nvidia's business.

items.title: This Stock Is Already Up 34% in 2025. Its Growth Is Getting a Big Boost From Amazon and Alphabet.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Pinterest's user base is largely untapped, with over 90% of its international users being undermonetized. However, partnerships with Amazon and Alphabet are helping to scale advertising demand and improve monetization, driving the stock's 34% year-to-date gains in 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights that Pinterest's international user base, which makes up over 50% of its total users, is currently undermonetized by over 90% compared to its overall average. However, the company's partnerships with Amazon and Alphabet are helping to scale advertising demand and improve monetization in these markets, driving the stock's 34% year-to-date gains in 2025.

Amazon is mentioned as one of the key partners helping Pinterest scale advertising demand and improve monetization, particularly in international markets where Pinterest was struggling to generate first-party advertising demand on its own.

Alphabet, the parent company of Google, is also mentioned as a key partner helping Pinterest scale advertising demand and improve monetization, particularly in international markets where Pinterest was struggling to generate first-party advertising demand on its own.
